core_concept: Human Body: Cells, Tissues, Digestion, Excretion
facts: The stomach is an organ made of several types of tissues, and each tissue is a group of similar cells that work together to digest food.
The small intestine absorbs nutrients from digested food into the blood because its inner surface has tiny finger-like bumps that capture the nutrients.
The kidneys are part of the excretory system; they filter waste from the blood to make urine, which leaves the body through the bladder and urethra.
Cells are the smallest living parts of your body; for example, your skin is made of skin cells, and these cells form a tissue that protects your body.
misconceptions: Students think that the stomach is the only organ that digests food, and that the small intestine just moves food along without doing anything important.
Students think that cells are only found in plants or in pictures, not in their own muscles, skin, and bones.
